name: CSBC
pi: White, Forest M. (MIT, contact); Michor, Franziska (co-PI)
research_focus: Quantitative systems biology of glioblastoma; tumor-neural-immune interactions in the GBM microenvironment; integrated computational modeling to identify therapeutic strategies
description: MIT/DFCI NCI U54 Center for Systems Biology of Glioblastoma (GBM). Investigates interactions between neurons, immune cells, and tumor cells to build validated computational models of the tumor microenvironment and identify therapeutic targets.

name: GBM
description: GBM study within the Break Through Cancer investigation.
tags: GBM GBM_BTC Break Through Cancer BTC

name: Griffith
description: Griffith investigation, also referenced as the endometriosis or CGR scope.

name: Impact
pi: Fortune, Sarah (Harvard, contact); Flynn, Joanne (U Pittsburgh, co-lead); Boom, Robert (Case Western, co-lead); Bryson, Bryan (MIT); Lauffenburger, Douglas (MIT); Shalek, Alex (MIT)
research_focus: Host-pathogen interactions in tuberculosis; characterizing protective vs. non-protective immunological responses to Mycobacterium tuberculosis to inform vaccine design
description: Multi-center NIH-funded consortium (Hi-IMPAcTB) studying host immune response to Mtb across mice, NHPs, and humans using multi-modal data from lungs and blood.

name: MetNet
pi: Kamm, Roger D. (MIT, contact); Shenoy, Vivek (co-PI); Friedl, Peter (co-investigator)
research_focus: Mechanical determinants of metastatic cancer colonization, dormancy, and outgrowth; mechano-adaptation of circulating tumor cells during extravasation and organ-selective colonization
description: MIT NCI U54 MetNet Center studying how mechanical and molecular stresses drive metastatic colonization, dormancy, and outgrowth across organs. Integrates engineered in vitro systems, in vivo models, and computational approaches to identify anti-metastatic therapeutic strategies.

name: PUBLISHED
description: Published project scope used by reporting workflows.

name: SRP
research_focus: Environmental health impacts of N-nitrosamines; hazardous chemical sensing, mutagenicity, cancer risk, and remediation of contaminated drinking water in affected communities (Wilmington, MA and Passamaquoddy Tribe)
description: MIT Superfund Research Program (P42) funded by NIEHS studying N-nitrosamines at the Olin Chemical Superfund Site and beyond. Integrates engineering, biology, and community engagement to sense contaminants, assess health risk, and develop water treatment technologies.
